audienceResearch: Public-page and category evidence only. GitHub is a developer platform for repositories, collaboration, CI, security, AI coding, documentation, enterprise governance, education, and community. Assumptions are marked in segment evidence.
researchSources: public: github.com homepage/footer navigation visible on 2026-07-04
public: GitHub category knowledge as a code hosting and collaboration platform
assumption: sample cohort covers unauthenticated visitors because no real accounts are used

audienceSegments.name: Open-source contributor
audienceSegments.evidence: Public platform vocabulary emphasizes repositories, docs, community, GitHub CLI/Desktop/Mobile, and developer ecosystem.

audienceSegments.name: Engineering team evaluator
audienceSegments.evidence: Public navigation includes Enterprise, Security, Copilot, Pricing, Team, and Professional Services.

audienceSegments.name: Learning developer
audienceSegments.evidence: Public navigation includes Education, Skills, Docs, Community Forum, and developer newsletter.

audienceSegments.name: Mobile checker
audienceSegments.evidence: GitHub Mobile is a public product path; mobile visitors may inspect links and trust cues.
jobs: check a repo or docs quickly
see if mobile browsing is usable

roles.role: open-source contributor deciding whether a project is worth a first PR
roles.past: I have wasted weekends on inactive repositories and now look for maintainers, docs, issues, and contribution signals before investing time.
roles.discovery: A search result sent me to GitHub while I was comparing tools.
roles.goal: Find whether GitHub helps me judge repository credibility and contribution paths quickly.
roles.lifeGoal: Build a public technical reputation without wasting years on dead projects.

roles.role: engineering manager evaluating team collaboration and security
roles.past: My team already uses several tools, and every platform decision creates migration and compliance risk.
roles.discovery: A colleague said GitHub might consolidate code review, security, and AI assistance.
roles.goal: Decide whether GitHub feels credible enough for a team evaluation.
roles.lifeGoal: Protect my team's delivery reputation by choosing stable engineering systems.

roles.role: junior developer learning where to start
roles.past: I know Git basics but product menus and enterprise language can make me feel like I do not belong yet.
roles.discovery: I clicked GitHub from a tutorial that said to create an account.
roles.goal: Find a clear beginner path without drowning in product categories.
roles.lifeGoal: Become employable without losing confidence to confusing tooling.

roles.role: startup CTO comparing code hosting and AI coding claims
roles.past: I need developer speed, but AI and security claims need proof before I stake my team on them.
roles.discovery: I opened GitHub after hearing about Copilot and enterprise security.
roles.goal: Understand whether the site gives enough proof to investigate further.
roles.lifeGoal: Protect my startup runway by choosing developer infrastructure that compounds.

ideaRules.idea: Show a clearer path for small teams before enterprise language takes over.
Connect beginner docs to a first visible repository action.
Pair AI claims with concrete safeguards and examples near the claim.
